findings.evidence: `rg -n "isCharacteristicVector_iff|isNegativeDefinite_iff"` shows the analogous predicate restatement `isCharacteristicVector_iff` is `@[grind =]`, while `isNegativeDefinite_iff` at lines 143-145 is unannotated.
findings.fix: Annotate it as `@[grind =] theorem isNegativeDefinite_iff ...` so automation can unfold the public predicate to `(-P.intersectionMatrix).PosDef` without exposing the definition body.
findings.issue: `isNegativeDefinite_iff` is the `_def`/characteristic restatement of `IsNegativeDefinite`, but it has no `@[grind =]` annotation.

findings.evidence: Lines 93-99 prove both projections of `P.intersectionForm.Nondegenerate`, but lines 101-105 expose only the left-kernel wrapper.
findings.fix: Add the parallel right-kernel theorem, e.g. `IsNegativeDefinite.eq_zero_of_intersectionForm_right (h) (hx : ∀ y, P.intersectionForm y x = 0) : x = 0 := h.intersectionForm_nondegenerate.2 x hx`.
findings.issue: The file exposes two-sided nondegeneracy but only provides the left-kernel eliminator `eq_zero_of_intersectionForm_left`; users with `∀ y, P.intersectionForm y x = 0` must project through the structure manually.

summary: The new surface is mostly focused, but the characteristic API is incomplete for automation and for the two-sided nondegeneracy it exposes.
